file_name: SmartRxSystemsInc_20180914_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11351705_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Distributor Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: Products shall mean the Company's Smart Pharm Assist Kiosk™ and associated documentation, inventions, software, application and processes.
perturbation.changed_text: Products shall mean only the Company's Smart Pharm Assist Kiosk™ hardware. All associated documentation, inventions, software, applications, and processes are specifically excluded.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Products' includes the Smart Pharm Assist Kiosk™ and associated elements. By changing the definition to include only the hardware and exclude the software, applications and processes, a contradiction is created when later sections discuss the marketing, maintenance, and functionality of the 'Products' since those activities inherently rely on the excluded components. This change introduces uncertainty about what the Distributor is actually responsible for marketing and supporting.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to market Company's Products in the Territory. Distributor agrees to include Company's trademarks, and the phrase "Authorized Company Distributor," on all literature, marketing collateral, stationery and business correspondence in which the Products or related services are mentioned.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Products' to exclude associated documentation, software, applications, and processes. The contradicted text requires the Distributor to market 'Company's Products.' Since the software, applications, and processes are essential components for the marketing and functionality of the Smart Pharm Assist Kiosk™, this exclusion creates a direct contradiction. The Distributor is obligated to market something ('Products') that are now defined in a way that makes effective marketing impossible because it lacks the software and supporting elements, which would directly impact the distributor's agreement to market the product.

perturbation.original_text: Territory shall mean Exclusivity for the state of Texas, USA, and Exclusive Distribution opportunities in all other states (with either the Company or Distributor), except the state of Florida.
perturbation.changed_text: Territory shall mean Exclusivity for the state of Texas, USA. Distribution opportunities in all other states is non-exclusive to the Distributor, and the company can appoint other distributors as well, except the state of Florida.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Territory' implies that in states other than Texas and Florida, either the Company or the Distributor can exclusively distribute. By removing the exclusive Distribution opportunities and making other distribution opportunities non-exclusive, we create a contradiction in the original context of this term. This change introduces uncertainty about the Distributors authority to distribute and it affects financial projections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exclusive Appointment. Company hereby appoints Distributor as Company's exclusive Distributor for the State of Texas, and Distributor accepts the appointment and agrees to represent the Products within the Territory. Company agrees that no other Distributor will be appointed in any other state as a Distributor unless it is either the Company or Distributor, save and except for the state of Florida.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'Exclusive Distribution opportunities in all other states (with either the Company or Distributor), except the state of Florida'. The changed text modifies this to 'Distribution opportunities in all other states is non-exclusive to the Distributor, and the company can appoint other distributors as well, except the state of Florida.' This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'Company agrees that no other Distributor will be appointed in any other state as a Distributor unless it is either the Company or Distributor, save and except for the state of Florida.' The original text and contradicted text allow for exclusive distribution rights by either the company or distributor, and the changed text makes the opportunity non-exclusive and allows for other distributors.

perturbation.original_text: Payment to the Distributor for the sale of the Products to Customers shall be as follows: (i) Company will pay Distributor (or its Assignee) Ten Percent (10%) of the Master Distributor Override generated by each Customer Kiosk Location. (Master Distributor Override ("MDO") shall mean Total Revenue per Customer Kiosk Location collected by SRXS from third party payors and patient cash payments). This 10% of the total revenue generated is payable only if the EBITDA is a positive EBITDA and exceeds the 10% of the revenue. In cases of a positive EBITDA but shortage of funds compared to the 10% of the revenue, Distributor will take less than 10%. If the EBITDA is negative, no payments will be made to Distributor. Additionally, it will be the sole responsibility of Distributor to make a full disclosure of the 10% MDO to all its clients.
perturbation.changed_text: Payment to the Distributor for the sale of the Products to Customers shall be as follows: (i) Company will pay Distributor (or its Assignee) Ten Percent (10%) of the Gross Profit generated by each Customer Kiosk Location. (Gross Profit shall mean Net Revenue per Customer Kiosk Location collected by SRXS from third party payors and patient cash payments, less the costs of goods sold). This 10% of the total revenue generated is payable regardless of the EBITDA. Additionally, it will be the sole responsibility of Distributor to make a full disclosure of the 10% Gross Profit to all its clients.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the distributor receives 10% of the MDO from a customer Kiosk location, only if EBITDA is positive. The modified text changes this condition so that the payment should be on Gross Profit, regardless of EBITDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment to the Distributor for the sale of the Products to Customers shall be as follows: (i) Company will pay Distributor (or its Assignee) Ten Percent (10%) of the Master Distributor Override generated by each Customer Kiosk Location. (Master Distributor Override ("MDO") shall mean Total Revenue per Customer Kiosk Location collected by SRXS from third party payors and patient cash payments). This 10% of the total revenue generated is payable only if the EBITDA is a positive EBITDA and exceeds the 10% of the revenue. In cases of a positive EBITDA but shortage of funds compared to the 10% of the revenue, Distributor will take less than 10%. If the EBITDA is negative, no payments will be made to Distributor. Additionally, it will be the sole responsibility of Distributor to make a full disclosure of the 10% MDO to all its clients.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the condition for payment to the distributor. The original text states that payment is based on Master Distributor Override (MDO) and is contingent on a positive EBITDA. The changed text alters the payment basis to Gross Profit, regardless of EBITDA. The contradicted text which is the same as the original text explicitly states that payment is only made if EBITDA is positive, thus creating a direct conflict with the modified text.
